basic_details.job_title: CSIR NIScPR Project Staff Recruitment 2026 – Walk in for 40 Project Associate, Project Scientist and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: CSIR-National Institute of Science Communication and Policy Research (CSIR-NIScPR)
basic_details.post_name: Project Associate-I (PAT-I), Project Associate-II (PAT-II), Project Assistant-II (PA-II), Senior Project Associate (SPA), Principal Project Associate (Principal PAT), Project Scientist-II (PS-II)
basic_details.short_information: CSIR-National Institute of Science Communication and Policy Research (CSIR-NIScPR), New Delhi has released the CSIR-NIScPR Project Staff Walk-in-Interview 2026 notification under Advt No. NIScPR/OLP/IV/2026-R&A for 40 posts across 19 research projects covering science policy, AI/ML, environmental science, science communication, and more on a contractual basis. Eligible Indian citizens holding Master's/B.Tech/BE/MCA/PhD qualifications can attend the Walk-in-Interview on 14 May 2026 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM at Vivekananda Hall, CSIR-NIScPR, New Delhi after downloading the detailed notification P

important_dates.reporting time: 09:00 AM to 11:00 AM (Late arrivals not entertained)

application_fee.details: No application fee is required. Candidates must report directly for the Walk-in-Interview with their completed application form and original documents.

age_limit.maximum_age: Maximum 35 Years (as on date of Walk-in-Interview)
age_limit.age_relaxation: SC / ST / OBC / EWS / PwBD as per Government of India / CSIR guidelines
age_limit.details: Project Associate-I (PAT-I) – 35 Years Project Associate-II (PAT-II) – 35 Years Project Assistant-II (PA-II) – 35 Years Senior Project Associate (SPA) – 40 Years Principal Project Associate (Principal PAT) – 40 Years Project Scientist-II (PS-II) – 40 Years

qualification.details: Nationality: Candidate must be a citizen of India.

salary_details.details: Project Associate-I (PAT-I) – ₹31,000/month + HRA (for NET/GATE/National exam qualified candidates); ₹25,000/month + HRA (for others) Project Associate-II (PAT-II) – ₹35,000/month + HRA (for NET/GATE/National exam qualified candidates); ₹28,000/month + HRA (for others) Project Assistant-II (PA-II) – ₹20,000/month + HRA Senior Project Associate (SPA) – ₹42,000/month + HRA Principal Project Associate (Principal PAT) – ₹49,000/month + HRA Project Scientist-II (PS-II) – ₹67,000/month + HRA

selection_process: Candidates must report at the NIScPR Reception Desk on 14 May 2026 between 9:00 AM and 11:00 AM (late arrivals will not be entertained). Selection will be based on: (a) Academic records
and/or (b) Performance in written test (if applicable)
and (c) Interview. CSIR-NIScPR reserves the right to cancel or withdraw the offer at any stage without assigning any reason.
how_to_apply: Download the official Application Format from the CSIR-NIScPR website: https://niscpr.res.in.
Fill in the prescribed application format with all required details (Name, DOB, Category, Qualification, Experience, etc.).
Affix a recent passport-size photograph and sign across it on the application form.

Bring the duly filled application form along with all original certificates and one set of self-attested photocopies (Age proof, Category certificate, Educational qualifications, Experience certificates, NET/GATE certificate if applicable).
Report at the Vivekananda Hall, CSIR-NIScPR, Dr. K. S. Krishnan Marg, Pusa Gate, New Delhi-110012 on 14 May 2026 between 09:00 AM and 11:00 AM.
important_instructions: Project staff engagement is on a contractual basis up to 31 March 2027 only, or co-terminus with the respective project. Under no circumstances shall the total tenure of project staff in CSIR-NIScPR or any CSIR laboratory exceed six years. Only candidates who have acquired the essential qualification and whose result has been declared (at least provisional certificate) are eligible to appear. Result-awaited / final semester candidates are not eligible where the minimum essential qualification is mandatory. Experience will be counted from the date after acquiring the minimum essential educational qualification. Dissertation/thesis work during academic period will not be counted as experience. Part-time, daily wages, visiting/guest faculty experience will not be counted towards valid experience. Mere fulfilling of minimum qualifications does not confer any right to selection. No upgradation of project staff shall be done in the same or new project. The engagement may be terminated at any time by giving one month's notice by either side. No TA will be paid to candidates for appearing in the Walk-in-Interview. No claim for permanent position shall be entertained. Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification. Number of positions may increase or decrease.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in CSIR‑NIScPR Project Staff Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: CSIR‑NIScPR has announced 40 project staff vacancies across 19 research projects for positions like Project Associate‑I/II, Project Assistant‑II, Senior Project Associate, Principal Project Associate and Project Scientist‑II.

faq.question: Q2. When and where is the CSIR‑NIScPR Project Staff Walk‑in‑Interview 2026?
faq.answer: The walk‑in‑interview will be held on 14 May 2026 at Vivekananda Hall, CSIR‑NIScPR, Dr. K.S. Krishnan Marg, Pusa Gate, New Delhi‑110012, and candidates must report between 9:00 AM and 11:00 AM.

faq.question: Q3. What are the essential qualifications for Project Associate‑I and Project Associate‑II posts?
faq.answer: Project Associate‑I requires MSc (Natural/Agricultural/Pharma sciences)/MVSc/Animal Sciences or BE/BTech/MBBS, while Project Associate‑II needs the same degrees plus 2 years’ R&D experience in industrial/academic/S&T organisations or a Master’s/Integrated Master’s in Engineering/Technology.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for CSIR‑NIScPR Project Staff 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 35 years for PAT‑I, PAT‑II and PA‑II and 40 years for Senior Project Associate, Principal Project Associate and Project Scientist‑II, with relaxations for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD as per CSIR/Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the monthly salary for CSIR‑NIScPR Project Staff posts?
faq.answer: Typical CSIR project pay scales are ₹31,000 + HRA for Project Associate‑I (₹25,000 + HRA without NET/GATE), ₹35,000 + HRA for Project Associate‑II (₹28,000 + HRA for others), ₹20,000 + HRA for Project Assistant‑II, ₹42,000 + HRA for Senior Project Associate, ₹49,000 + HRA for Principal Project Associate and around ₹67,000 + HRA for Project Scientist‑II, depending on qualification and project norms.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for CSIR‑NIScPR Project Staff Walk‑in‑Interview 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee; eligible candidates can directly appear for the walk‑in with the prescribed application form and documents.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for CSIR‑NIScPR Project Staff Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection will be based on academic records, and/or performance in a written test (if held) and a walk‑in interview; only candidates reporting within the scheduled time with complete documents will be considered.

faq.question: Q8. How long is the tenure for CSIR‑NIScPR project staff positions?
faq.answer: Engagement is purely contractual, generally up to 31 March 2027 or till project completion, and the total project‑staff tenure in CSIR (any lab) cannot exceed six years; there is no claim for regularisation.

faq.question: Q9. Which documents should I carry for the CSIR‑NIScPR Project Staff walk‑in 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must bring a filled application form, one set of self‑attested photocopies and originals of age proof, caste/EWS/PwBD certificate, educational degrees and marksheets, experience certificates, NET/GATE proof (if applicable) and a recent passport‑size photograph.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply for CSIR‑NIScPR Project Staff Walk‑in‑Interview 2026?
faq.answer: Download the application format from niscpr.res.in → Jobs, fill it completely, affix a photograph, and report in person at Vivekananda Hall, CSIR‑NIScPR, New Delhi on 14 May 2026 between 9:00 and 11:00 AM with the filled form and required documents; no online application or advance submission is required.
